question: Which New York City middle schools located in Queens District 26 had (1) at least 50 offers to specialized high schools on specialized high school admissions test in every school year from 2015-19, and (2) are named after scientists. In what year was the research institute founded and first directed by the older of these two scientists? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = 'datagov/2015-2016-shsat-admissions-test-offers-by-sending-school/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
offers = pd.to_numeric(df['Count of Offers'], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[offers >= 50].copy()
lookup = {row["Feeder School Name"]: (row["Feeder School DBN"], int(pd.to_numeric(row['Count of Offers'], errors="coerce"))) for _, row in filtered.iterrows()}
answer = {}
for school in ["J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur", "M.S. 158 Marie Curie", "J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne", "J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan"]:
    dbn, count = lookup[school]
    answer[school] = f"{dbn}, {count} offers" if True else dbn
answer['and 23 others'] = "..."
1.subquestion: Which NYC middle schools had at least 50 SHSAT offers to specialized high schools in school year 2015-16?

5.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2016-2017-school-locations/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
schools = ["J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur", "J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne", "M.S. 158 Marie Curie", "J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan"]
filtered = df[df["LOCATION_NAME"].isin(schools)].copy()
filtered["GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE"], errors="coerce")
assert set(filtered["GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE"].dropna().astype(int)) == {26}
lookup = dict(zip(filtered["LOCATION_NAME"], filtered["ATS_SYSTEM_CODE"]))
answer = {school: f"{lookup[school]}, District 26" for school in schools}
5.subquestion: Which of the 21 schools with >= 50 SHSAT offers in all 4 years are located in Queens District 26?

6.fact: Louis Pasteur (27 December 1822 - 28 September 1895) was a French chemist, pharmacist, and microbiologist renowned for his discoveries of the principles of vaccination, microbial fermentation, and pasteurization. He was a scientist who made groundbreaking contributions to medicine and biology.
6.subquestion: Was Louis Pasteur a scientist, and when was he born?

7.fact: Maria Salomea Sklodowska-Curie (7 November 1867 - 4 July 1934), better known as Marie Curie, was a Polish and naturalised-French physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity. She was a scientist who won two Nobel Prizes.
7.subquestion: Was Marie Curie a scientist, and when was she born?

8.fact: Nathaniel Hawthorne (July 4, 1804 - May 19, 1864) was an American novelist and short story writer. His works often focus on history, morality, and religion. He was not a scientist but a literary figure.

9.fact: The Pasteur Institute is a French non-profit private foundation dedicated to the study of biology, micro-organisms, diseases, and vaccines. It is named after Louis Pasteur. The institute was founded on 4 June 1887 and inaugurated on 14 November 1888.
9.subquestion: In what year was the research institute named after <the older scientist from nodes 6-7: Louis Pasteur> founded?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: SHSAT offers SY2015-16 >= 50 -> 27 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Node 2: SHSAT offers SY2016-17 >= 50 -> 27 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Node 3: SHSAT offers SY2017-18 >= 50 -> 28 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Node 4: SHSAT offers SY2018-19 >= 50 -> 23 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Intersection of nodes 1-4: Schools meeting criterion all 4 years -> 21 schools
Node 5 (School Locations): Filter to Queens District 26 -> 4 schools (Pasteur, Hawthorne, Curie, Ryan)
Node 6 (Wikipedia Louis_Pasteur): Pasteur was a scientist, born December 27, 1822
Node 7 (Wikipedia Marie_Curie): Curie was a scientist, born November 7, 1867
Node 8 (Wikipedia Nathaniel_Hawthorne): Hawthorne was NOT a scientist (novelist)
Note: George J. Ryan has no Wikipedia article and cannot be verified as a scientist
Filter: Scientists only -> Pasteur (born 1822), Curie (born 1867) = 2 scientists
Superlative: Older (earlier birth year) -> Pasteur (1822) vs Curie (1867) -> Louis Pasteur is older
Node 9: Louis Pasteur founded the Pasteur Institute in 1887 and was its first director until his death
